interpretation: The original benchmark objective is substantially satisfied for dense Qwen through the stable I2_SR path. It is not fully complete because the requested TL2 path is not quality-preserving for learned row-scale checkpoints; current TL2 collapses row scales to one tensor scale.

rows.evidence: Qwen2.5-1.5B repaired hidden-MSE step-5000: ternary=197/197, scales=197, dense=True; Qwen2.5-0.5B step-1000: ternary=169/169, scales=169, dense=True; Qwen2.5-1.5B KL row-scale dense-head step-5000: ternary=196/196, scales=196, dense=True
rows.item: 1. Fix FSDP ternary export and re-export 1.5B step-5000

rows.evidence: Qwen2.5-1.5B repaired step-5000: 5 prompts; Qwen2.5-0.5B step-1000: 5 prompts
rows.item: 2. Run eval/prompt suites on repaired 1.5B and complete 0.5B

rows.evidence: 12/12 finite artifacts=True; FP WikiText=13.901475466625362; naive PTQ WikiText=3813121.80332679; best row-scale WikiText=38.58006540148406; models=6, tasks/model=10, samples/model=22382; FP mean=0.644169; PTQ mean=0.348671; row-scale mean=0.499459

direct dense/scalar writers exist; I2_SR candidate gate=True; active default gate=True; TL2 row-scale one-scale error=1.9042302114103853; row-fp16 design error=0.00019744640689756221 at 1.23046875 MiB; TL2 ready=False; TL2 current row-scale error=1.9042302114103853; TL2 failed checks=6
rows.gap: Dense GGUF and row-scale I2_SR/I2_S CPU inference exist, but TL2 is not quality-preserving for learned row-scale checkpoints until row/group-scale metadata and kernels are implemented.
rows.item: 5. Convert repaired checkpoints into GGUF/TL2/I2_S and run CPU inference

rows.evidence: I2_SR PPL=38.8477, file=1211.3 MiB, prompt=211.67 tok/s, decode=19.07 tok/s; Q4_K_M file=940.4 MiB; RSS contexts=[512, 2048, 8192, 32768]; I2_SR decode=19.065496 tok/s; Q4_K_M decode=16.013125 tok/s
rows.item: 6. Measure Xeon speed, prompt throughput, RSS, model size, and quality loss

tl2_failed_blockers: Existing TL2 collapses learned row scales to one scale and exceeds the allowed relative output RMS error.
`transform_to_tl2` is data-only and recomputes a scalar scale instead of carrying checkpoint row/group scales.
The converter still derives one scalar `np.max(abs(x))` scale and writes a single sidecar scale tensor.
`GGML_TYPE_TL2` storage has no explicit row/group-scale count or dedicated row-scale TL2 qtype.
`ggml_bitnet_transform_tensor` still builds metadata for one scale, not one scale per row or row group.
No row-scale TL2 GGUF has passed PPL, throughput, and RSS-style evidence.
